ops.title: ccx vcs ship commits onto whatever lineage the working copy is parked on — verify it against the release line (origin/main) before shipping, and don't read ship's exit 0 as CI green
ops.body:
`ccx vcs ship` commits onto the current working-copy lineage and pushes that bookmark — it does not check that you're on the repo's release line. In cc-transcript (2026-07-10) the checkout was parked on a divergent local `platform-2.0` branch; ship pushed there ("CI no-run") and a `v10.3.0` tag minted from that lineage failed the release workflow's `verify-tag-on-main` guard (thankfully before publish).

**Why:** jj-colocated repos often sit on an in-flight feature lineage; session-start `git log` shows that lineage's history, which looks exactly like main's.

**How to apply:** Before any ship/tag in a repo I didn't just clone: `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main HEAD` (or `jj log -r 'main@origin | @'`) and check where the release tags actually point (`git log -1 <latest-tag>`). Read ship's output line — "CI no-run" means nothing was watched; exit 0 is not CI green. Related: [[jj-colocated-use-jj-not-git-stash]], [[gh-run-watch-target-by-workflow-and-sha]].
